meta.title: LEAN Score
meta.cff_info:
LEAN Score

Reitsma FJ et al., 2025 (European Stroke Journal)

DOI: 10.1177/23969873251350882

Full citation: Reitsma FJ, van Kuijk SMJ, Werring DJ, Banerjee G, Cordonnier C, Kaaouana O, Puy L, Biffi A, Viswanathan A, van Oostenbrugge RJ, Staals J, Rouhl RPW. Development and external validation of the LEAN score to predict late seizures after intracerebral haemorrhage. European Stroke Journal. 2025. doi:10.1177/23969873251350882.

What it predicts

Late seizures after primary intracerebral haemorrhage (ICH): seizures occurring >7 days after ICH onset (the model is derived among patients surviving the first 7 days).

Applicable population

  • Primary ICH survivors beyond day 7 (derivation cohort excluded deaths within 7 days).
  • No known epilepsy / antiepileptic drug use at baseline (per paper’s exclusion criteria).

Factors and Points (Total: 0–6)

  • (L) Lobar ICH:
    • No: 0 points
    • Yes: 2 points

    How to assess: Determine hemorrhage location from baseline CT or radiology report. “Lobar” = lobar cortical/subcortical location (frontal/parietal/temporal/occipital), as opposed to deep/infratentorial. Paper notes lobar location is more straightforward than cortical involvement.

  • (E) Early seizures (≤7 days):
    • No: 0 points
    • Yes: 2 points

    How to assess: Any clinically recognized seizure occurring within the first week after ICH onset (during acute admission or early post-discharge).

  • (A) Age <65 years at ICH onset:
    • Age ≥65: 0 points
    • Age <65: 1 point

    How to assess: Use age at the time of ICH onset; cut-off is 65 years.

  • (N) Any neurosurgical intervention:
    • No: 0 points
    • Yes: 1 point

    How to assess: Mark “Yes” if any neurosurgical procedure was performed (examples listed include drainage, decompression, combined drainage+decompression, hemicraniotomy, ventricular drainage).

Risk estimates by LEAN score (paper-reported; end of follow-up)

Corresponding risk of developing late seizures from day 7 to end of follow-up by exact score (0→6): 0.7%, 1.6%, 8.8%, 22.0%, 29.8%, 43.5%, 100%.

Note: These timepoint risks are derived from digitized Kaplan–Meier curves (Figure 1) using your provided WebPlotDigitizer export, to enable month-by-month curve plotting in the calculator. The paper also reports overall cohort cumulative risk benchmarks (e.g., 7.2% at 1 year; 10% by 8 years) for the derivation cohort.

Clinical utility

LEAN is a pragmatic bedside score using readily available clinical variables and CT location, intended to stratify late-seizure risk after ICH.

Additional study information

Category Details
Derivation cohort South Limburg (Netherlands) ICH registry (2004–2009), n=781; 78 (10%) developed late seizures.
Follow-up Median follow-up 4.8 years (range 0–12.6). Overall cumulative late-seizure risk: 7.2% at 1 year and 10% by 8 years (no LS reported beyond 8 years).
Model type Cox proportional hazards model; simplified into integer points (LEAN).
Discrimination (derivation) Harrell’s c-statistic 0.80 (95% CI 0.78–0.86).
External validation Validated in 3 cohorts (Lille, Boston, CROMIS-2); LEAN remained a significant predictor across cohorts.
Limitations highlighted Derivation cohort is older (2004–2009); some predictors missing in validation cohorts (e.g., early seizures unavailable in CROMIS-2; volume unavailable in Boston), potentially affecting precision/generalizability.
meta.authors: Reitsma FJ, van Kuijk SM, Werring DJ, et al.
meta.clinical_utility: Introduction: Predicting the occurrence of late seizures after intracerebral haemorrhage may help in making clinical decisions about treatment. Currently, the CAVE score is the best performing risk score.
meta.outcome: Development and external validation of the LEAN score to predict late seizures after intracerebral haemorrhage

predictors.name: Lobar ICH
predictors.assess: From the diagnostic head CT (or radiology report), determine whether the hemorrhage location is lobar (cortical/subcortical lobes) vs non-lobar (deep/infratentorial). Use the radiology report classification if available.

predictors.name: Early seizures (≤7 days)
predictors.assess: Determine whether the patient had any clinically recognized seizure within the first 7 days after ICH onset (during admission or early post-discharge window).

predictors.name: Any neurosurgical intervention
predictors.assess: Whether any neurosurgical procedure was performed for the ICH (e.g., drainage, decompression, hemicraniectomy, ventricular drainage). If any occurred, score “Yes.”

provenance.notes: Cumulative+COSY parsed from CFF curve equations; complete meta from paper; verify vs live form.
